audit_trail.name: Greyscale Conversion
audit_trail.description: Converted BGR input to single-channel greyscale. Colour is irrelevant for ink-on-paper OCR and removing it reduces processing cost by 3×.

audit_trail.name: Bilateral Denoising Filter
audit_trail.description: Applied edge-aware bilateral filter (d=9, σColor=75, σSpace=75). Suppresses paper grain and scanner noise while preserving the sharp ink-stroke boundaries that OCR depends on.

audit_trail.name: CLAHE Contrast Enhancement
audit_trail.description: Applied Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization (clipLimit=2.0, tile=8×8) to recover faded ink strokes and compensate for lighting gradients across the document surface.

audit_trail.name: Adaptive Gaussian Binarization
audit_trail.description: Converted to binary using local Gaussian-weighted thresholding (blockSize=15, C=10). Each pixel is compared to its 15×15 local neighbourhood average, isolating ink strokes from yellow staining and uneven lighting that would defeat global thresholding.

audit_trail.name: OCR — Tesseract LSTM (PSM 6, OEM 3)
audit_trail.description: Recognised text at 49% mean confidence. 29% of tokens high-confidence (≥80%). Tokens below 40% confidence dropped from extracted_text to eliminate scanner-border noise.

audit_trail.name: Metadata Extraction
audit_trail.description: Regex entity extraction found 0 date(s), 3 person(s), 1 place(s). Document type: unrecognised.

audit_trail.name: Annotated Image
audit_trail.description: Drew per-word bounding boxes colour-coded green/yellow/red by OCR confidence tier (≥80 / 50-79 / <50).

audit_trail.name: Layout Map
audit_trail.description: Morphological dilation (kernel 15×5, 2 iterations) merged characters into paragraph blobs; contours mark layout regions.

audit_trail.name: Table Detection
audit_trail.description: Morphological line extraction + numeric-alignment heuristic. Found 1 table(s). Note: borderless tables require deep-learning models (future work).

numeric_facts: Photograph by ReJLANDER. Proofs, Prints, 103. d. 4 sy

ai_analysis.artifact_profile:
Physical Format
Materiality: Printed paper
Condition: Poor OCR legibility; text is fragmented and contains numerous typographical errors, suggesting a low-quality scan or a worn original document.
Legibility Score (1-5): 2
ai_analysis.provenance:
Temporal Origin: Mid-to-late 19th Century (indicated by the reference to "The Late Prince Consort")
Geographic Origin: Edinburgh, Scotland
Author: James Wood
Role: Publisher
Document Genre
ai_analysis.anthropological_matrix:
Positionality
Intended Audience
Dominant Power Dynamic: Commercial/Capitalist (the promotion and sale of intellectual and artistic goods)
Notable Silences: The laborers/photographers (except Rejlander), the subjects of the landscape views, and the working-class population of Edinburgh.
ai_analysis.taxonomy:
Locations: Edinburgh; Calton Hill; Edinburgh Castle; Princes Street; Craigleith Quarry; Loch Katrine; Loch Achray; Liverpool; London.
Key Figures: James Wood; Rejlander; The Late Prince Consort.
Thematic Tags
Indigenous Lexicon: None
ai_analysis.validity_bias:
Proximity
Bias Level (1-5): 4 (Promotional/Commercial bias)
Primary Value
ai_analysis.executive_summary:
Elevator Pitch: This document is a publisher's advertisement catalog from James Wood in Edinburgh, listing various available works including landscape photography, chemistry manuals, and photographic journals. It serves as a commercial directory for Victorian-era printed and photographic media.
Key Takeaway: The document illustrates the commercial intersection of photography, science, and tourism in 19th-century Scotland.
Next Steps: Research the specific publication dates of the "Manual of Chemistry" and the works of the photographer Rejlander to establish a precise chronology.
